name: Christopher Rose
focus: Instrument engineering • Remote sensing hardware • Planetary mission support
bio: Christopher Rose is an instrument engineer at Northern Arizona University, where he works within a planetary science research group focused on developing advanced remote sensing instruments for space exploration. As part of a team led by Dr. Christopher Edwards, he contributes to the design, testing, and implementation of instrumentation used to study planetary surfaces, particularly through thermal infrared spectroscopy and rover-scale sensing systems . His work supports NASA and international missions by helping translate scientific goals into functional hardware and data collection systems. In addition to his academic research role, Rose has experience contributing to applied engineering and technical development projects, reflecting a background that bridges hands-on engineering with planetary science applications.
